catalog_row.Paper: A small quarterly projection model of the US economy
catalog_row.ModelType: linear stationarized semi-structural quarterly projection model with financial-real linkages
catalog_row.Category: semi-structural projection model; monetary policy; financial-real linkages
catalog_row.KeyFeatures: output gap; hybrid inflation equation; Taylor-type policy rule; dynamic Okun law; stochastic potential output and NAIRU; equilibrium real-rate process; BLT lending-tightness block; cross-correlated shocks
catalog_row.RawPDF: raw/mmb_papers/A small quarterly projection model of the US economy.pdf

status_row.paper_title: A small quarterly projection model of the US economy
status_row.raw_pdf_path: raw/mmb_papers/A small quarterly projection model of the US economy.pdf
status_row.notes: First-pass bilingual derivation; OCR has damaged symbols around definitions and inflation prose; no appendix normalization; raw PDF body not read; Dynare runtime validation not performed.

validation.command: English derivation has all eight required sections and includes (F1)
English and Chinese F-number counts match
validation.details: 68 total F-number references in each file; 18 unique equation numbers, F1-F18

unresolved_issues: Targeted PDF review should verify the damaged output-gap definition, equilibrium real-rate process weights, and equilibrium BLT random-walk subscript.
The paper conceptual system has more latent states and shocks than the stationarized MMB implementation.
Original level-model steady-state logic is not fully source-stated.
Cross-correlated shocks are documented from the paper but not fully encoded in the stationarized implementation excerpt.
Dynare runtime validation was intentionally not performed.
